student_description: This student is navigating highly sensitive trauma literature and exhibits polarized reactions, ranging from deep vulnerability and fear of being triggered to defensive dismissiveness. They uniquely require intense psychological safety, trauma-informed facilitation, explicit choices in their engagement, and private spaces to process their reactions away from peers.

criteria.rationale: Addressing dismissiveness or fear of triggers in public can exacerbate defensive behaviors, whereas private check-ins build trust.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly offers or initiates a private, one-on-one conversation (e.g., before class, between classes) to discuss the student's reactions or upcoming content.

criteria.rationale: This student has a specific fear of being 'caught off guard' by traumatic literature and needs to know what is coming to prepare emotionally.
criteria.check: The tutor provides explicit details or previews about the day's lesson and upcoming reading material to remove the element of surprise.

criteria.rationale: Shifting the focus from the trauma itself to how to help others empowers the student and provides a safe, constructive way to engage with the text.
criteria.check: The tutor guides the discussion toward exploring supportive scenarios, such as how to help friends in dangerous situations or how to act as an ally.
